turns.name: ORAL ARGUMENT OF WILLIAM T. CANNON
turns.text: Mr. Chief Justice, and may it please the Court: The Government characterizes Plaintiff's action as one growing cut of assault and battery, an intentional tort for which tha Government has not raised sovereign immunity. It also claims Plaintiff's action is barred by the Feres doctrine. The court of appeals decided both that the Feres doctrine does not apply to this case, and that Plaintiff's cause of action was not barred by the intentional tort exception. State and federal courts across this land have entertained successful negligence actions under Section 448, notwithstanding the presence of gross, violent and often homicidal conduct on the part of■intervening tort feasors.
